text: What if evolution isn't a smooth climb, but a series of sudden leaps? Punctuated equilibrium suggests that species evolve in rapid bursts, separated by long periods of stability. This challenges traditional Darwinian views, which see evolution as a constant, gradual process. Fossil records show sudden appearances of species, supporting this theory. So, evolution might be a dance of chaos and order, not just a straight line. Curious about the mysteries of evolution? Follow for more insights!
